hero.title: Authentic Turkish Cuisine
hero.subtitle: Experience the flavors of Istanbul in the heart of Dresden

about.title:
A quiet homage
to İstanbul.
about.body: Three generations, a single kitchen. We cook for the few guests we welcome tonight — with spices we toast ourselves and bread we knead by hand. No spectacle. Just taste.
about.lede: A small kitchen by the Elbe — run with the same patience our mother used when she stirred her pilav in Adana.

story.title: How it started.
story.p1: In 1987 Mehmet Yıldız opened the doors of a small kitchen by the river. He cooked what his mother had cooked in Adana: long-braised aubergine, hand-cut lamb, bread he kneaded himself.
story.p2: Today three generations work in the same kitchen. We still toast our spices ourselves — every morning, before the first guest arrives. We buy in small quantities, from the same four farmers we've worked with for twenty years.
story.p3: What hasn't changed: bread is kneaded by hand. The tomatoes come from a garden in Saxony. The yoghurt is set in our kitchen. And we cook for the few guests we welcome that evening — not for a thousand.

quote.text: We don't cook for many. We cook for the table that arrives tonight.
quote.author: Mehmet Yıldız

hand.title: By hand
hand.body: Bread, yoghurt, mezeler — everything is made in our kitchen. No ingredient we couldn't prepare ourselves reaches the plate.

patience.title: With patience
patience.body: Some dishes braise for eight hours. We take the time because flavour takes time — and because a guest can taste it.

place.title: Of place
place.body: Our tomatoes grow in Saxony. Our lamb comes from the Erzgebirge. A Turkish kitchen in Dresden — and you can taste it.

contact.title:
We look forward
to your visit.
contact.lede: Reservations by phone or email. For larger tables and special occasions just write — we'll set the evening for you.

reservation.body: Please reserve by phone or email. We usually respond the same day.

menu_preview.title: Three classics.
menu_preview.lede: A small selection from tonight's card.

adana.name: Adana Kebap
adana.desc: Hand-cut lamb, pul biber, over charcoal.

imam.name: İmam Bayıldı
imam.desc: Slow-braised aubergine, garden tomato, olive oil.

menu.title:
What lands
on the table tonight.
menu.lede: A small, hand-curated list. It shifts when the market asks it to.

cart.title: Your table
cart.empty_lede: Browse the menu and add what speaks to you.

order.title:
Confirm
your table.
order.lede: We start cooking the moment payment lands. Leave a number in case the kitchen needs to call.

empty.title: Your table is empty.
empty.lede: Pick a few things from the menu first. We'll be here.

summary.note: VAT included. Tipping at your discretion.

type.label: How would you like to order?

table.label: Table number
table.help: You'll find it on the QR card at your table.

email.label: Email (optional)
email.help: We'll send the confirmation by email.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

canceled.title: Payment canceled.
canceled.body: No worries — your selection is still here. Send the order again whenever you're ready.

success.title:
Thank you.
We're cooking.
success.lede: We've received your order and started preparing it. A confirmation email is on its way.
success.missing_session: We can't find this order. Please reach out to the restaurant.

toasts.success: Order placed — the kitchen is on it.
toasts.error_generic: We couldn't send the order. Please try again.

p1.title: First kitchen
p1.body: Mehmet opened the doors of a small kitchen by the Elbe. He cooked what his mother had cooked in Adana.

p2.title:
Three generations,
one hand
p2.body: Today we knead the bread between three of us. The gesture hasn't changed.

p3.title:
Saxon
tomatoes
p3.body: The same four farmers. For twenty years. Small, personal, precise.

p4.title:
This evening,
this table
p4.body: We cook for the guests who arrive tonight. Not for a thousand. For you.

atmosphere.title:
A small kitchen
by the Elbe.
atmosphere.lede: Warm light, quiet voices, the scent of freshly toasted cumin.

lieferando.title:
Want it delivered?
Through Lieferando.
lieferando.body: We cook in the restaurant. For delivery to your door, our kitchen partners with Lieferando — the same menu, the same hands.

footer.tagline: Turkish cuisine from Dresden — by hand, without spectacle.
